data.title: Al Habtoor Group Initiates International Arbitration Proceedings Against Lebanese Republic
data.description:
AL HABTOOR GROUP:

  • INITIATES INTERNATIONAL ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS AGAINST LEBANESE REPUBLIC UNDER APPLICABLE BILATERAL INVESTMENT TREATY

  • REQUEST FOR ARBITRATION TRANSMITTED THROUGH INTERNATIONAL CENTRE FOR SETTLEMENT OF INVESTMENT DISPUTES (ICSID) IN WASHINGTON DC, AND LEBANESE REPUBLIC HAS BEEN FORMALLY NOTIFIED IN ACCORDANCE WITH ESTABLISHED PROCEDURES

  • STEP FOLLOWS GROUP’S PROLONGED GOOD‑FAITH EFFORTS TO RESOLVE THE MATTER AMICABLY, ALL OF WHICH HAVE BEEN EXHAUSTED WITHOUT A CONSTRUCTIVE OUTCOME

  • GROUP SUFFERED SIGNIFICANT LOSSES DUE TO LEBANESE REPUBLIC’S PROLONGED ACTIONS AND FAILURE TO PROTECT ITS INVESTMENTS

data.title: Mergers and acquisitions
data.description:
The following bids, mergers, acquisitions and disposals were reported by 1430 GMT on Thursday:

** Warner Bros Discovery shareholders on Thursday backed the company's proposed $110 billion merger with Paramount Skydance , but cast an advisory vote against executive compensation plans tied to the deal.

** Atlas Holdings said it has no intention to bid for UK construction materials supplier BRCK Group after it was denied sufficient access to company information and management ahead of a looming takeover code deadline.

** Portugal's government on Thursday asked Air France-KLM and Germany's Lufthansa to submit binding bids for a minority stake in flag carrier TAP, setting up a tight contest between the two European airline groups after closely matched initial offers, the only two it had received.

** Czech power utility CEZ is preparing to spin off its non-production assets and sell up to 49% of the new unit to investors, a move that could help fund the government's goal to take full control of the company's power generation business.

** European broadcaster RTL's takeover of pay-TV firm Sky Deutschland is expected to lead to significant job cuts, with a three-digit number of positions likely to be affected, according to two sources familiar with the matter.

** Spanish renewable power company Acciona Energia has received several non-binding offers for a package of 360 megawatts (MW) of Spanish wind assets, sources familiar with the matter told Reuters.

** Italian bank UniCredit holds a stake of 8.72% in Generali , the insurer's Chairman Andrea Sironi said as he listed the holdings of investors at its annual general meeting (AGM).

** Franco-Italian eyewear group EssilorLuxottica said it had acquired Faro, a small Italian company specialised in high-precision technologies for eyewear manufacturing.

** Swedish private capital group EQT is exploring a takeover of Japanese price comparison website operator Kakaku.com , a person with knowledge of the matter told Reuters.

** Private equity firm Apollo is nearing a deal to acquire the auto interiors business of France's Forvia , Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

** U.S. investment firm Bain Capital is seeking to sell a stake of at least 40% in Bridge Data Centres (BDC) in a deal that would value the Singapore-based data infrastructure builder at $5 billion, two people with knowledge of the matter said.

data.title: Baltic index edges down from over 4-month high on lower capesize, panamax rates
data.description:
The Baltic Exchange's dry bulk freight index, which monitors rates for ships carrying dry bulk commodities, edged down ending a 14-session winning streak, as rates for capesize and supramax vessel segments were lower.

  • The main Baltic index , which tracks rates for capesize, panamax and supramax vessels, edged 2 points lower, or 0.1%, to 2,673. It hit its highest level in more than four months on Wednesday.

  • The capesize index (.BACI) lost 41 points, or 0.9% to 4,315.

  • Average daily earnings for capesize vessels (.BATCA), which typically transport 150,000-ton cargoes, including iron ore and coal, decreased $368 to $35,634.

  • Iron ore prices eased on Thursday, as investors weighed prospects of a growing supply of the key steelmaking ingredient against higher costs stemming from the prolonged Iran conflict.

  • The panamax index (.BPNI) was down 6 points, or around 0.3%, to 1,965.

  • Average daily earnings for panamax vessels (.BPWT), which usually carry 60,000 to 70,000 tons of coal or grain, dipped by $62 to $17,682.

  • The supramax index (.BSIS) gained 38 points, or 2.6%, to 1,522, its highest since early December 2023.

  • Iran flaunted its tightened grip over the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday with video of its commandos storming a huge cargo ship, after collapse of peace talks that Washington had hoped would open the world's most important shipping corridor.

  • This, even as, U.S. President Donald Trump vowed to step up the U.S. blockade of Iranian trade by sea.

data.title: IndiGo gets DGCA warning over Dec 2025 airfares; no penalty imposed
data.description:
IndiGo has received a warning letter from the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) dated April 21, 2026, in connection with airfares it charged in December 2025 when temporary domestic airfare caps were in place.

The regulator’s communication pertains to compliance with fare limits that were in place during the specified period. The DGCA reviewed pricing practices and flagged concerns related to adherence to these temporary caps.

However, the airline stated that the warning letter does not have any significant impact on its financials or operations. There has been no disruption to services or changes to its business outlook following the regulator’s communication.

The DGCA has acknowledged that IndiGo has completed corrective actions in response to the issue. These measures include processing refunds where required, addressing concerns raised during the review of fare structures.

Importantly, the regulator has not imposed any penalty, restriction, or sanction on the airline. The absence of punitive action indicates that the matter has been resolved through corrective steps taken by the company.

The development relates specifically to a past period of fare regulation and does not reflect ongoing pricing practices. IndiGo continues to operate without any regulatory constraints arising from this issue.

The warning letter serves as a formal communication highlighting compliance expectations, while also recognising the airline’s remedial actions in addressing the concerns identified by the DGCA.

data.title: American Express beats estimates on premium member growth, but shares fall
data.description:
Revenues increased 11% year-on-year to $18.91bn, or 10% on an FX-adjusted basis, to top the consensus forecast of $18.61bn, as total billed business grew 10% to $428.0bn.

Amex said growth was mainly driven by higher spending from card members, as well as increased net interest income and strong card fee growth.

In an interview with Reuters, chief financial officer Christophe Le Caillec said overall retail spending had risen by 11% year-on-year during the quarter, with luxury retail spending in particular up 18%.

Net income rose 15% to $2.97bn, with earnings per share up 18% at $4.28, comfortably beating the $4.00 consensus estimate.

Chairman and chief executive Stephen Squeri labelled it a "very strong start" to 2026. "Card Member spending grew 9 percent FX-adjusted, the highest quarterly growth in three years, driven by strong demand and engagement with our premium products," he said.

Looking ahead, Amex kept on to revenue and profit guidance for the full year, expecting 9-10% top-line growth and EPS of $17.30-17.90.

"With our differentiated Membership Model, fuelled by our premium Card Members, world-class partners, and the innovations and services delivered by our talented colleagues, we are confident in our ability to deliver sustainable long-term growth," Squeri said.

The stock was down 2.2% at $326.22 by 1516 BST. Shares have risen around 10% over the past month to close at $333.09 on Wednesday, its highest since 26 February.

data.title: Rising Prices to Drive Up U.K.'s Inflation-Linked Debt Costs — Market Talk
data.description: Higher inflation is expected to push up borrowing costs for U.K. inflation-linked bonds and overall borrowing expenses, Union Bancaire Privee's Peter Kinsella says in a note. Around 24% of the U.K.'s debt stock is inflation-linked and is vulnerable to rising inflation, Kinsella says. Data Wednesday showed U.K. annual consumer-price inflation rose to 3.3% in March as the Middle East conflict caused a jump in energy prices. The real yield on the September 2035 inflation-linked gilt last trades at 1.474%, having traded below 1.3% just prior to the start of the war in Iran, Tradeweb data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)
